items.title: GPT-5.5
items.author: rd
items.summary: GPT-5.5 is an advanced AI model by Amazon, excelling in tasks like coding and research. It's praised for efficiency and intuitive use. It's seen as a significant step forward in AI capabilities.

items.title: An update on recent Claude Code quality reports
items.author: mfiguiere
items.summary: Recent updates show Anthropic resolved two quality issues in Claude Code, and they continue monitoring for further problems. The quality of outputs has improved since the fixes.

items.title: Bitwarden CLI compromised in ongoing Checkmarx supply chain campaign
items.author: tosh
items.summary: Bitwarden CLI was compromised in a Checkmarx supply chain attack; users should uninstall the package and secure their credentials. Immediate action is critical to prevent further security breaches.

items.title: US special forces soldier arrested after allegedly winning $400k on Maduro raid
items.author: nkrisc
items.summary: A US special forces soldier was arrested for allegedly using classified information to win $400,000 betting on the Maduro capture operation. The soldier, Gannon Ken Van Dyke, made bets on Polymarket before the operation. He has been charged with violating national security laws.

items.title: Familiarity is the enemy: On why Enterprise systems have failed for 60 years
items.author: adityaathalye
items.summary: Enterprise systems have struggled for decades due to rigid structures failing to adapt to dynamic market demands, echoing Moltke's insight that no plan survives first contact with the unknown. This rigidity often leads to failure.

items.title: MeshCore development team splits over trademark dispute and AI-generated code
items.author: wielebny
items.summary: The MeshCore development team split due to a trademark dispute and the use of AI-generated code. Key figures involved are Scott Powell and Liam Cottle, who pursued closed-source business models. The team's division led to significant project fragmentation.

items.title: Using the internet like it's 1999
items.author: joshuablais
items.summary: In 1999, the internet featured early browsers like Netscape and Internet Explorer, with significant events including the rise of Napster and the launch of Blogger. The browser war was dominated by Microsoft, and Google was emerging as a competitor. Oldweb.today lets users experience this era.

items.title: Show HN: Agent Vault – Open-source credential proxy and vault for agents
items.author: dangtony98
items.text: Hey HN! Today we're launching Agent Vault - an open source HTTP credential proxy and vault for AI agents. Repo is at https://github.com/Infisical/agent-vault, and there's an in-depth description at https://infisical.com/blog/agent-vault-
items.summary: Agent Vault is an open-source HTTP proxy and vault for AI agents, designed to prevent credential exfiltration while allowing agents to securely access external resources. It mitigates risks from prompt injection by keeping credentials separate from the agent. The project aims to enhance security for AI agents by managing credentials through a dedicated proxy.

items.title: Used La Marzocco machines are coveted by cafe owners and collectors
items.author: mitchbob
items.summary: Used La Marzocco machines are highly sought after by cafe owners and collectors due to their reputation for quality and performance. Their desirability has grown, making them valuable second-hand investments. Their classic design and reliable operation continue to attract interest.

items.title: Isopods of the world
items.author: debesyla
items.summary: There are over 10,000 described species of isopods worldwide, divided into eleven suborders, with around 4,500 species in marine environments, 500 in freshwater, and 5,000 on land. The isopods are highly diverse, found in all ecosystems from oceans to terrestrial habitats.

items.title: A Renaissance gambling dispute spawned probability theory
items.author: sohkamyung
items.summary: A Renaissance gambling dispute led mathematicians to develop probability theory for risk assessment. This dispute involved dividing an interrupted game's winnings. The theory laid the foundation for modern statistics.

items.title: Girl, 10, finds rare Mexican axolotl under Welsh bridge
items.author: codezero
items.summary: A 10-year-old girl found a rare Mexican axolotl under a bridge in Wales. The critically endangered creature was rescued and named Dippy D. The discovery highlights the species' vulnerability.

items.title: Our newsroom AI policy
items.author: zdw
items.summary: Newsrooms use AI tools under strict oversight, with humans making all editorial decisions. Ars Technica uses AI for visual material but maintains human creative direction. The Investigative Journalism Foundation views AI as a tool to support their mission.

items.title: WireGuard for Windows Reaches v1.0
items.author: zx2c4
items.summary: WireGuard for Windows has reached version 1.0, offering a secure VPN solution for Windows users. This release marks the official maturity of the open-source software. It provides robust encryption and performance for secure connections.

items.title: GPT-5.5: Mythos-Like Hacking, Open to All
items.author: rs_rs_rs_rs_rs
items.summary: GPT-5.5 significantly improves vulnerability detection, achieving top performance and faster login times compared to previous models. It reduces persistence in giving up scenarios by half. It outperforms older versions and Opus 4.7 on benchmarks.

items.title: AI run store in SF can't stop ordering candies and paying women less.
items.author: fragmede
items.summary: An AI-run store in San Francisco orders candies excessively and pays female employees less. The store's founders are exploring ethical implications of AI management. The store targets AI-interested tourists.

items.title: Study shows removing exposure to plastic from our food chain yields quick result
items.author: xbmcuser
items.summary: After a week, a study found that reducing plastic exposure led to a 44% decrease in phthalates and over 50% in bisphenols, including BPA and BPS, compared to a control group. This reduction was achieved by avoiding plastic food packaging and utensils. The study was conducted by researchers from the University of Western Australia.

items.title: Investigation uncovers two sophisticated telecom surveillance campaigns
items.author: mentalgear
items.summary: Two advanced telecom surveillance campaigns exploited long-known vulnerabilities in mobile networks, using commercial tools to track targets. The campaigns mimicked operator identities and manipulated signaling protocols. Researchers linked these attacks to specific infrastructure nodes.
